hero_section.title: Automated charts with room for detailed analyses.
hero_section.content: We build reporting systems that present complex results in a condensed, intuitive way – while still allowing your experts to drill down into details when needed.

card.subtitle: From data to decisions
card.title: Reporting that shows the essentials at first glance.
card.content: Within our online reports it is possible to carry out highly individualised and detailed analyses – without losing the big picture. Standard charts for management, flexible views for experts.
card.caption: Explore the sections below for use cases, dashboard types and data infrastructure.

why_section.title: From complex results to clean stories.
why_section.subtitle: Reporting systems translate detailed research and tracking results into clear views. The objective: show the essentials at first sight while keeping all detail available in the background.

items.title: Structured data as a base
items.text: Structured data and database management are the basis of detailed and stable reporting – especially when projects run across years and markets.

items.title: Condensed & individualised views
items.text: We condense complex results into clear figures and filters, tailored to different user groups such as management, experts and local teams.

items.title: Continuous monitoring
items.text: Campaigns, satisfaction and response rates can be monitored continuously, tracking both current status and desired targets.

dashboards_section.title: Tailored reporting for different user groups.
dashboards_section.subtitle: The same underlying data can be presented very differently, depending on whether you are steering at board level, analysing details or running a local team.
dashboards_section.toggle_hint: Toggle between different dashboard types to see how the same underlying data can support board-level steering, expert analysis and local management.

modes.label: Management overview
modes.intro: At overview level, standard KPIs and trends are shown in a compact way, so management can see status and direction at a glance.
cards: Executive dashboards with a small set of core KPIs.
Time series views for tracking movements over multiple waves.
Traffic-light indicators for targets and thresholds.

modes.label: Expert detail view
modes.intro: Detail views allow experts to filter by segment, time, product and channel, while keeping definitions and filters transparent.
cards: Flexible filters for markets, products, segments and time.
Drill-down from total to specific sub-groups or questions.
Download options for detailed tables and microdata extracts.

modes.label: Local & branch view
modes.intro: Branch and local views highlight what is relevant locally, comparing each unit to benchmarks so teams can steer concretely.
cards: Simple dashboards for non-analysts with only a few KPIs.
Comparison to network average and predefined peer-groups.
Optional comment and action fields for local follow-up.

governance_section.title: Reporting systems that play nicely with your infrastructure.
governance_section.subtitle: Reporting is only sustainable when data flows, access rights and definitions are clear. We design systems that can live for years, not just for a single project.

items.title: Data pipelines & interfaces
items.text: We set up stable exports and APIs from reporting systems to your BI tools, data warehouse or data lake – with clear schemas and IDs.
items.extra: This includes standardised refresh cycles and monitoring of data flows.

items.title: Access control & roles
items.text: Role-based access ensures that sensitive metrics and customer-related data are only visible to authorised users.
items.extra: Views can be aligned with internal structures such as countries, regions or branches.

items.title: Definitions & documentation
items.text: KPIs, filters and survey variables are documented in a way that both analysts and non-specialists can understand.
items.extra: We provide simple data dictionaries, filter descriptions and FAQs inside or next to the dashboards.
